definition: For DMS-like datasets with a wild-type reference, describe each variant by its mutated positions. Use distance to the nearest mutated training position or region, or normalized sequence identity/Hamming similarity, as the SPECTRA axis. Contiguous-region holdouts become one discrete version of this broader positional novelty axis.
example_usage: python sequence_hamming_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --sequence-col sequence

citations.title: NABench: Large-Scale Benchmarks of Nucleotide Foundation Models for Fitness Prediction

evidence.summary: The paper contrasts random cross-validation with contiguous cross-validation over wild-type sequence blocks to test extrapolation to unseen mutational regions.
The paper reports a performance gap between random CV and contiguous CV, emphasizing sensitivity to OOD sequence regions.
The paper tracks mutation depth across DMS assays, supporting mutation count as an additional sequence novelty descriptor.

limitations: Plain Hamming similarity ignores structural context and epistasis.
Contiguous position holdouts require a meaningful wild-type coordinate system.
SELEX libraries may not have a wild-type reference, making contiguous CV inappropriate.
quality_gates: Separate DMS and SELEX datasets before choosing this axis.
Report whether sequences are aligned and equal length.
Audit both mutation count and nearest mutated-region distance where possible.
